model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase MLP capacity to improve representation quality under the fixed 256-step budget, while keeping the existing optimizer and schedule unchanged for a clean architecture-focused test.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switch to AdamW to decouple weight decay from adaptive moments, which often improves generalization for larger MLPs at the same learning-rate schedule and step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Remove batch normalization to reduce optimization noise and train/eval-stat mismatch in the short fixed-step regime, which can help MLPs on flattened CIFAR inputs.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Revert the two changes that degraded loss and return to the previously strongest configuration before testing new ideas, restoring batch normalization and Adam.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Add mild label smoothing to reduce overconfidence and improve generalization in the fixed short-budget regime without changing model capacity or optimizer dynamics.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Back out the harmful label smoothing and slightly lower the peak learning rate to improve stability for the larger MLP under cosine decay within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Continue LR tuning in the same direction after a gain at 8e-4, testing whether a slightly lower peak rate further improves short-budget convergence.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The trend indicates better validation loss as LR is reduced, so test another small decrement to 6e-4 while keeping all other successful settings fixed.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss kept improving as learning rate decreased, so testing 5e-4 is a low-complexity continuation of the same successful direction.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Return to the better LR and test slightly weaker L2 regularization, since the current short-budget run likely benefits from less underfitting while keeping optimization stable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Probe between the two strongest nearby rates (6e-4 and 7e-4) to target a local optimum while leaving all other settings unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6.5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Undo the harmful LR increase and test an intermediate weight decay between the two best nearby values to refine regularization without changing architecture or schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase effective network width by raising FC_HIDDEN above BASE_CHANNELS*16, creating a higher-capacity MLP that may reduce validation loss within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 320","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the prior effective capacity and test a lower Adam beta2 to increase adaptation speed in the 256-step regime, which can improve short-horizon optimization.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With beta2 reduced to 0.99 yielding a gain, test a slightly smaller learning rate to improve stability and possibly lower validation loss further in the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation improved when slightly lowering LR with beta2=0.99, so test another small decrease to check if the optimum is still lower.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore the better learning-rate setting and test a slightly weaker weight decay to see if a small reduction in regularization improves validation loss at the new optimizer dynamics.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3.5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3.6e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Current setting is nearly tied with the best result, so a tiny local sweep around weight decay is a low-risk final refinement attempt.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3.6e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Revert to the slightly better local optimum from the immediate weight-decay sweep to maximize final verified validation loss performance.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}
